entradas.titulo: El SAT de la 4T duplica la recaudación por fiscalizar a grandes empresas
entradas.resumen: El monto recaudado por el cobro de adeudos fiscales a grandes compañías en 2025 es tres veces el gasto ejercido por la Secretaría de Comunicaciones y Transportes en un año.

entradas.titulo: México llevará el acero como primer tema comercial a la mesa del T-MEC
entradas.resumen: Durante el último año, el comercio de acero entre ambos países se convirtió en uno de los puntos más sensibles de la relación bilateral.

entradas.titulo: México amplía su ventaja comercial con Canadá con exportaciones por más de 22,000 mdd
entradas.resumen: Gobiernos y sector privado impulsan misiones comerciales y acuerdos empresariales para ampliar el intercambio entre México y Canadá.

entradas.titulo: Citi México: Financiaremos proyectos de energía del plan de infraestructura
entradas.resumen: Luis Brossier afirma que empresas ya dialogan con CFE sobre generación eléctrica y que el banco apoyará con financiamiento cuando se asignen los proyectos.

entradas.titulo: ¿Por qué suben los precios del petróleo a pesar de la liberación récord de reservas?
entradas.resumen: Los precios también están subiendo debido a una nueva ola de ataques iraníes dirigidos a objetivos energéticos del Golfo.

entradas.titulo: BBVA: Los proyectos del Plan México serán exitosos y rentables si se ejecutan bien
entradas.resumen: Eduardo Osuna, director general de BBVA México, dijo que la banca está lista para financiar los proyectos del Plan México, siempre que cuenten con permisos, fuentes de pago claras y una adecuada asignación de riesgos.

entradas.titulo: EU investiga a México y otras economías por sobrecapacidad manufacturera
entradas.resumen: La USTR indicó que el proceso en el marco de la Sección 301 analizará si las políticas industriales de estos países son irrazonables o discriminatorias y si restringen el comercio de Estados Unidos.

entradas.titulo: Plan México busca ampliar el crédito pyme, pero el reto sigue siendo el primer financiamiento
entradas.resumen: El plan busca ampliar el financiamiento a pequeñas empresas con garantías de Nafin y mayor colocación bancaria, pero la falta de historial crediticio es la principal barrera para el primer préstamo.

entradas.titulo: BanCoppel: “La cartera de autos de CI Banco nos trajo 27,000 clientes nuevos”
entradas.resumen: Carlos López-Moctezuma dice que el banco tomó el control de la cartera en febrero, contrató cobranza especializada y prevé un alza temporal en la morosidad mientras los clientes migran al nuevo sistema de pago.

entradas.titulo: La AIE libera 400 millones de barriles de crudo de reservas, el mayor desbloqueo de su historia
entradas.resumen: La Agencia Internacional de Energía busca compensar la pérdida de suministro por el cierre efectivo del Estrecho de Ormuz y mitigar los efectos inmediatos de la perturbación de los mercados.

entradas.titulo: El SAT va por comerciantes, manufactureras, mineras y servicios financieros
entradas.resumen: El fisco publicó 80 tasas efectivas pagadas en 2022 y 2023; los contribuyentes que pagaron menos ISR que el promedio de su actividad, ya tienen un mensaje en su Buzón Tributario para ponerse al tanto.

entradas.titulo: CEO de Nubank : Trump ha sido "positivo" para el sector financiero en EU
entradas.resumen: Nubank obtuvo en enero una aprobación condicional para operar como banco en Estados Unidos, el mayor mercado financiero del mundo, y ahora está a la espera de la licencia definitiva.

entradas.titulo: Multiva: “104 fideicomisos quedaron fuera; no cumplían nuestro apetito de riesgo”
entradas.resumen: Tamara Caballero, directora de Multiva, dijo que tras adquirir casi 3,000 fideicomisos de CI Banco, depuraron contratos con apoyo de IA y listas de riesgo, lo que permitió sumar clientes y elevar su captación.

entradas.titulo: Banco BASE: “Acusaciones de lavado de dinero fueron una sacudida para los bancos”
entradas.resumen: Julio Escandón, director general del banco, afirma que tras los señalamientos de EU contra instituciones financieras, se optó por frenar la captación de nuevos clientes y reforzar sus controles de cumplimiento.

entradas.titulo: Guerra deja más dinero público por crudo, pero menos por subsidios a gasolinas
entradas.resumen: Por mayores precios del petróleo, los ingresos de México pueden acelerarse, pero el alza en los precios de gasolinas borrará los ingresos extras.

entradas.titulo: Bankaool: Nuestros sistemas antilavado nacieron fuertes
entradas.resumen: Juan Antonio Pérez Simón, director general del banco, dijo que tienen un área de PLD fuerte que les blinda de señalamientos contra el lavado de dinero y espera mantener crecimiento de las utilidades.

entradas.titulo: México perfila revisión del T-MEC con foco en reglas de origen, cadenas y seguridad
entradas.resumen: El proceso de consultas reunió a empresas, cámaras industriales, organizaciones agropecuarias, sindicatos y especialistas académicos para construir una postura antes de iniciar conversaciones formales.

entradas.titulo: Inflación en México repunta a 4.02% en febrero y queda fuera del rango objetivo de Banxico
entradas.resumen: El alza de alimentos y servicios mantuvo presiones en los precios durante febrero, mientras el componente subyacente —clave para Banxico— se ubicó en 4.50% anual.

entradas.titulo: Nu: Tenemos más de 2 millones de clientes en el sur; el crédito es transformador
entradas.resumen: Armando Herrera, CEO de Nu México, dijo que de los más de 13 millones de clientes que tienen, un 14% está en la región de menos inclusión financiera.

entradas.titulo: Revolut: "En México hay millones de personas que no están cómodas con su banco"
entradas.resumen: Juan Miguel Guerra, director del banco, ve una gran oportunidad en la insatisfacción con los servicios financieros tradicionales y apuesta por ofrecer más productos en una sola app para competir con la banca en México.

entradas.titulo: Gold Set for Weekly Drop as Mideast War Keeps Oil Prices High
entradas.resumen: Gold rose on Friday, but remained on track for a second weekly decline as the war in the Middle East kept oil near $100 a barrel.

entradas.titulo: Australian Retirement Trust CIO on Investment Strategy
entradas.resumen: Ian Patrick, CIO at the Australian Retirement Trust, discusses the investment opportunities ahead as Australia’s pension industry aims to deepen their push into overseas markets. He speaks with Haidi Stroud-Watts and Avril Hong from the sidelines of the Australian Superannuation Investment Summit. (Source: Bloomberg)

entradas.titulo: Asian Stocks Trim Earlier Losses, Oil Rally Cools: Markets Wrap
entradas.resumen: Asian stocks pared early declines and S&P 500 futures rose after the US issued a second temporary waiver allowing purchases of Russian oil to help curb surging prices and ease inflation concerns.

entradas.titulo: Gas Shortage, Harsh Summer Lure Bulls to India Power Utilities
entradas.resumen: Coal India shares rose for the third successive day to end at their highest level in over 16 months.

entradas.titulo: Xiaomi’s Memory, EV Woes Hand Short Sellers $1.8 Billion Payout
entradas.resumen: Short sellers betting against Xiaomi Corp. are reaping big gains, as surging memory prices and weakening electric-vehicle sales cloud the company’s earnings outlook.

entradas.titulo: Asia Markets at Risk Due to Oil Dependency: BCA Research
entradas.resumen: BCA Research's Garry Evans says the extent of the pressure on Asian markets "comes down to the dependence on oil." He tells Bloomberg Television that investors should look at places "that have quite a lot of energy companies in their indexes," such as Australia, Canada and Mexico. (Source: Bloomberg)

entradas.titulo: India Asks China for Urea as War-Induced Gas Crunch Bites
entradas.resumen: India has asked China to allow the sale of some urea cargoes as the war in the Middle East curtails the nation’s gas supplies, threatening fertilizer production in the agricultural powerhouse.

entradas.titulo: Iron Ore Set for Biggest Weekly Gain In a Year on CMRG’s Curbs
entradas.resumen: Iron ore headed for its biggest weekly gain in over a year, after China’s state-backed buyer moved to expand restrictions on BHP Group products.

entradas.titulo: Tencent, Alibaba Due as OpenClaw AI Mania Spreads in China
entradas.resumen: The hype around artificial intelligence software OpenClaw has heightened the interest in Tencent Holdings Ltd. and Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. earnings and how the agent might drive growth and innovation across multiple technology industry sectors.

entradas.titulo: China’s Train to North Korea Lacks Passengers on Maiden Voyage
entradas.resumen: Rush-hour was a notch louder at Beijing Station on Thursday, as passengers scurried to board the first K27 train service in six years. Destination: Pyongyang, capital of reclusive North Korea.

entradas.titulo: Hedge Fund Regal Hit by 93% Crash in Australian Biotech Stock
entradas.resumen: Sydney hedge fund Regal Partners Ltd. was dealt a fresh blow after Immutep Ltd., an Australian biotech company it backs, wiped out most of its A$582 million ($412 million) market value on Friday when it halted trials of a lung cancer drug.

entradas.titulo: Trump and Iran Strike Defiant Tone as Oil Markets See Little Relief
entradas.resumen: President Donald Trump and Iran’s new supreme leader both struck defiant tones on the 13th day of the war, offering little relief to energy markets despite fresh US efforts to curb oil prices.

entradas.titulo: Oil Drillers Resort to Trucks as Key California Pipe Idled
entradas.resumen: Oil drillers in central California have resorted to the costly and cumbersome alternative of trucking crude barrels 50 miles after the shut down of a refinery and idling of a key pipeline cut off outlets for their products.

entradas.titulo: Australian Gold Miner Northern Star Sinks as Output Guidance Cut
entradas.resumen: Northern Star Resources Ltd. sank the most in six years after the gold miner cut output guidance due to problems at a processing mill.

entradas.titulo: Chinese Power Giant Sheds Light on Arcane Part of Credit Markets
entradas.resumen: A Chinese energy-construction giant is inadvertently spotlighting an arcane corner of the credit markets, where investors seek to use a technicality to potentially more than double their fixed-income returns.

entradas.titulo: Australia Pension Eyes Private US Assets Before NYC Meetings
entradas.resumen: One of Australia’s biggest pension funds, Rest Super, is on the hunt for more private markets investments in the US, underscoring the strong appetite from the country’s fast-growing A$4.5 trillion ($3.2 trillion) sector that’s on a roadshow across three American cities.

entradas.titulo: Brent Oil Trades Near $100 as Iran Vows to Keep Hormuz Closed
entradas.resumen: Brent oil traded near $100 a barrel following one of the most volatile trading weeks ever, with investors bracing for more upheaval as Iran pledged to keep the Strait of Hormuz effectively shut.

entradas.titulo: Brookfield Seeks Around $800 Million Loan for World Freight LBO
entradas.resumen: Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. is seeking a loan of around $800 million to back its proposed purchase of air cargo specialist World Freight Co., according to people familiar with the matter, adding to a recent wave of acquisition financings in Asia.

entradas.titulo: Magnitude 5.3 Earthquake Hits Turkey, USGS Says
entradas.resumen: An earthquake with a magnitude of 5.3 struck 16 kilometers east of Erbaa, Turkey, according to the United States Geological Survey.

entradas.titulo: China Consumption Start to Year Seen as Worst Ever Outside Covid
entradas.resumen: China’s consumer spending may have booked the worst start to any year outside the pandemic, highlighting the challenge to a government that has targeted a bigger role for domestic demand.

entradas.titulo: Iran’s Strait of Hormuz Toolkit: Drones, Missiles, and Mines
entradas.resumen: A breakdown of the tools Iran has to maintain pressure on Trump.

entradas.titulo: Iran’s New Supreme Leader Vows to Continue Blocking the Strait of Hormuz
entradas.resumen: Tehran escalates Gulf strikes and warns of future attacks on U.S. bases in the region.

entradas.titulo: Trump’s Last-Gasp Foreign Policy
entradas.resumen: Ill-considered overreach in Iran and elsewhere signals imminent decline.

entradas.titulo: Trump’s Efforts to Defuse the Oil Spike Aren’t Working
entradas.resumen: Big reserve releases, promises of escorts, and insurance can’t convince the market that the crisis will be over soon.

entradas.titulo: A Film of Unimaginable Horror and Unrelenting Humanity
entradas.resumen: “The Voice of Hind Rajab” is an indictment of the international system that has failed Gaza.

entradas.titulo: How to (Not) Be a Pacific Power
entradas.resumen: Washington has yet to recognize that mobility is an essential part of regional security.

entradas.titulo: The War That Cracked the Postwar Order Illusion
entradas.resumen: Conflict in Ukraine has shattered the myths sustaining the Western coalition.

entradas.titulo: The Irresistible Urge to Invoke World War III
entradas.resumen: Forget the inflated comparisons. Neither the war in the Middle East nor the Russian invasion of Ukraine ever signaled a global conflagration.

entradas.titulo: Countries Agree to Historic Emergency Oil Reserve Release
entradas.resumen: IEA member states will distribute 400 million barrels of oil to ease the Iran war-caused supply crunch.

entradas.titulo: Trump’s New Cyber-First War Strategy
entradas.resumen: From Iran to Venezuela, Trump has fully embraced offensive cyberoperations.

entradas.titulo: How South Africa Is Navigating the Iran War
entradas.resumen: Pretoria’s awkward dance between Washington and Tehran.

entradas.titulo: The Major Local Election That France’s Far Right Might Just Win
entradas.resumen: A year before presidential elections, the National Rally has a chance of capturing Marseille, a historic hub of immigration.

entradas.titulo: Gaza’s Reconstruction Must Begin With Its Youth
entradas.resumen: A better future for Palestine depends on psychological recovery.

entradas.titulo: Mojtaba’s Selection Is a Sign of Political Exhaustion
entradas.resumen: In choosing Khamenei’s son, the regime has imposed exactly what many Iranians feared.

entradas.titulo: Why Haven’t the Houthis Fired?
entradas.resumen: The rational calculus, for the moment, is that death to America can wait.

entradas.titulo: Trump Promised to Tear Up the Regime-Change Playbook. He Is Now Following It.
entradas.resumen: Exaggerate the threat and underestimate the consequences.

entradas.titulo: Trump Likely to Confront Democratic Wall on Iran War Funding
entradas.resumen: An emergency military spending request to continue the Iran strikes may soon be sent to U.S lawmakers.

entradas.titulo: Iran Threatens to Stop Oil From Leaving the Middle East
entradas.resumen: Trump has warned that doing so will only escalate the U.S. military’s war effort.

entradas.titulo: State Department Refuses Former Staffers’ Help in Middle East Evacuation
entradas.resumen: Around 250 State Department employees could assist with efforts.

entradas.titulo: Why China Might Have a Role to Play in Iran
entradas.resumen: Diplomatic messaging suggests Beijing’s potential for mediation in the war.

entradas.titulo: "Mordí la fibra óptica": Héroe de Rusia que luchó en solitario 68 días cuenta cómo llegó hasta sus camaradas en Donbass
entradas.resumen: Serguéi Yaráshev, de 21 años, retuvo una posición durante más de dos meses cerca del poblado de Gríshino.

entradas.titulo: Reportan supuestos avistamientos de Pie Grande
entradas.resumen: Ninguno de los siete episodios, reportados todos en Ohio (EE.UU.), fue captado por cámaras.

entradas.titulo: FOTO deja en evidencia la paralización del paso por el estrecho de Ormuz
entradas.resumen: Medios iraníes difundieron una foto de vista aérea del estrecho de Ormuz, donde decenas de barcos esperan para pasar, en medio de las tensiones por la agresión de EE.UU. e Israel contra Irán. La nación persa bloqueó casi por completo la ruta marítima por donde circula alrededor de 20 % de todo el petróleo que se comercia en el mundo, lo que causó el alza de precios en el mercado energético.

entradas.titulo: "Estaremos todos en el culo profundo": Fico advierte a la OTAN sobre el envío de tropas a Ucrania
entradas.resumen: "Alguien podría activar el Artículo 5", alertó el jefe del Gobierno eslovaco.

entradas.titulo: Reportan muertos y heridos en un ataque de Pakistán contra Afganistán
Venezuela firma un acuerdo con dos gigantes energéticos europeos
¿Cómo impacta la agresión a Irán en los 5 bancos centrales más sólidos de América Latina?
entradas.resumen: El panorama energético afecta de distintas formas y plantea escenarios diversos que responden a la posición de los países como exportadores o importadores de crudo.

entradas.titulo: Cuba anuncia la próxima liberación de decenas de personas encarceladas
"¡Basta ya de este circo!": Opositor de Macron exige no dar "ni un euro más para Zelenski"
entradas.resumen: Florian Philippot criticó el inminente encuentro entre el presidente francés y el líder del régimen de Kiev.

entradas.titulo: País africano endurece las penas contra homosexuales
entradas.resumen: Una nueva legislación clasifica la homosexualidad junto con la necrofilia y la zoofilia.

entradas.titulo: Iranian missile strikes residential area in northern Israel
entradas.resumen: Video from northern Israel captured the moment an Iranian missile impacted in a residential area early Friday morning.

entradas.titulo: Thousands rally in Athens protesting US-Israeli attacks on Iran
entradas.resumen: Protesters in Athens have marched to the US Embassy to condemn the US-Israeli attacks on Iran.

entradas.titulo: Netanyahu says Israeli strikes killed Iranian nuclear scientists
entradas.resumen: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said several Iranian nuclear scientists were killed in Israeli strikes.

entradas.titulo: Michigan synagogue ramming an ‘act of targeted violence’: FBI
entradas.resumen: The FBI says it is treating a vehicle ramming at a Michigan synagogue as an attack on the Jewish community.

entradas.titulo: Iran war live: Trump says war going ‘well’ as Gulf under wave of attacks
entradas.resumen: Israeli strikes kill dozens more in Lebanon, including 5 children among nine people killed in attack on village of Arki.

entradas.titulo: UN fact-finding mission warns of continued human rights abuses in Venezuela
entradas.resumen: In an address to the UN Human Rights Council, experts warned that the 'machinery' of repression was 'mutating'.

entradas.titulo: Iran envoy says Tehran will keep Strait of Hormuz open
entradas.resumen: Iran’s UN envoy Amir Saeid Iravani said Tehran will not close the Strait of Hormuz and remains committed to freedom.

entradas.titulo: Qatar rejects attempts to ‘drive wedge’ with US over gas pause
entradas.resumen: Qatari official denies political motives for decision to halt LNG production following Iranian strikes.

entradas.titulo: Ex-rapper Balendra Shah sweeps to power in Nepal landslide election victory
entradas.resumen: Rastriya Swatantra Party, founded just four years ago, set to dominate new parliament with near two-thirds majority.

entradas.titulo: US military announces rescue effort after fuelling aircraft crashes in Iraq
entradas.resumen: No deaths have been reported so far, but the military said the crash involved two aircraft, one of which landed safely.

entradas.titulo: Air attacks target bases of Iran-backed forces in Iraq
entradas.resumen: Air attacks have targeted military bases across Iraq belonging to Iran-backed forces.

entradas.titulo: Netanyahu says Israel ‘stronger than ever’ in first speech since Iran war
entradas.resumen: Prime minister says US-Israeli war prevented Iran from moving its nuclear and ballistic projects underground.

entradas.titulo: Fuel shortages bring queues, protests around the world
entradas.resumen: Rising fuel prices have caused long queues and protests around the world as the US-Israeli war on Iran rages.

entradas.titulo: Advocates push for major probe as US boat strikes in Latin America kill 157
entradas.resumen: The Inter-American Commission on Human Rights is set to hold first hearing on US's alleged extrajudicial killings.

entradas.titulo: Can AI in military operations really be ethical?
entradas.resumen: We examine concerns about AI’s role in military operations and the broader ethical challenges facing tech companies.

entradas.titulo: Taiwan cheering row overshadows China quarterfinal at Asian Cup
entradas.resumen: Taiwan face China in a tense Women's Asian Cup football quarterfinal, where political tensions will be at the fore.

entradas.titulo: How will the war on Iran impact the US economy?
entradas.resumen: Outcomes depend on how quickly oil tankers can return to the Strait of Hormuz, experts said.

entradas.titulo: Does President Trump have an exit strategy for the war with Iran?
entradas.resumen: United States officials are issuing contradictory statements on the war's timeline and objectives.

entradas.titulo: Iran vows to make Trump pay for ‘grave miscalculation’ if US escalates war
entradas.resumen: Ali Larijani responds to US threat to hit Iran's power infrastructure, saying the 'whole region will go dark'.

entradas.titulo: ICC prosecutor opens probe into Belarus over deportations to Lithuania
entradas.resumen: The prosecutor's office said the crimes being investigated include deportation and persecution through deportation.

entradas.titulo: US sanctions 2 firms, 6 individuals for funding North Korean weapons
entradas.resumen:
The United States has imposed sanctions on two companies and six individuals for their roles in operations that used information technology workers in foreign countries to raise money for North Korea.
The Treasury Department said the schemes, orchestrated by the North Korean government, had systematically defrauded US businesses and generated revenue to fund North Korea’s weapons programmes, including nearly US$800 million in 2024.
The department also accused workers associated with North Korea...

entradas.titulo: California’s Governor Newsom says no imminent threat despite warning of Iran drone attack
entradas.resumen:
California Governor Gavin Newsom said there was no imminent threat to the state, despite a warning from the FBI that Iran could send drones to the west coast in retaliation for war.
Newsom said drone issues “have always been top of mind”.
“We’ve been aware of that information. … It’s all about a posture of preparedness for worst-case scenarios,” the governor said on Wednesday.
The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) recently warned police departments about Iran and a possible California...

entradas.titulo: US military refuelling plane crashes in Iraq, rescue efforts are under way
entradas.resumen:
An American military refuelling plane went down in Iraq and rescue efforts were under way, US Central Command said on Thursday.
The KC-135 aircraft was part of the operation against Iran but the crash was not due to hostile fire or friendly fire, the military said in a statement.
US Central Command, which oversees the Middle East, said two aircraft were involved and that one landed safely and the other went down in western Iraq. It described the latter as “a loss”.
A US official, who spoke on...

entradas.titulo: Serbian president confirms his country owns Chinese supersonic missiles after photo leak
entradas.resumen:
Serbia’s president confirmed late on Thursday that his country possesses Chinese supersonic surface-attack cruise missiles, after photos of the weapons attached to Serbian fighter jets leaked online.
Photos, which began circulating on social media and defence blogs earlier this week, showed missiles believed to be Chinese-made CM-400AKG cruise missiles attached to Serbia’s Russian-made MiG-29 planes.
“We have things we do not show,” President Aleksandar Vucic told Serbia’s national broadcaster...

entradas.titulo: Netanyahu threatens Iran’s new supreme leader, defends joint assault with US
entradas.resumen:
Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday issued ⁠a veiled threat to kill Iran’s new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei even as he acknowledged that Israel’s joint air war with the US may not lead to a collapse of Tehran’s clerical government.
Holding his first press conference since the start of the war, ‌the Israeli leader said that Iran was “no longer the same” after nearly two weeks of bombardment and that Tehran had suffered blows to its elite Revolutionary Guards Corps and Basij...

entradas.titulo: Iran war signals return to world of hierarchy without order
entradas.resumen:
The war in Iran is not just another Middle Eastern crisis. It is a window into what world politics looks like when rules are weakest and power is most concentrated. Washington has spoken in the language of surrender and coercion, European governments have called for restraint and respect for international law, and Asian powers have scrambled to keep energy flows moving through a Strait of Hormuz that has nearly ground to a halt.
What this reveals is not a functioning rules-based order. It is a...

entradas.titulo: 1 dead, 2 injured in shooting at Virginia’s Old Dominion University, police say
entradas.resumen:
ROTC (Reserve Officers’ Training Corps) students in an Old Dominion University classroom subdued and killed the shooter who killed one person and wounded two others, the FBI announced on Thursday.
Dominique Evans, special agent in charge of the FBI’s (Federal Bureau of Investigation) Norfolk field office, said at a news conference that the suspect identified by authorities as Mohamed Bailor Jalloh shouted “Allahu akbar” before the shooting. Then the ROTC students stopped him, showing “extreme...

entradas.titulo: Suspect shot dead after crashing truck into Michigan synagogue
entradas.resumen:
A suspect crashed ⁠his truck into the hallway of a Detroit-area synagogue where children were attending preschool on Thursday and was shot dead in a confrontation with security staff, with no one else seriously injured, authorities said.
The attack unfolded during a recent surge in US antisemitic incidents and in a period of heightened security concerns around Jewish and Muslim places of worship since US and Israeli forces launched air strikes on Iran on February 28, sparking an intensifying war...

entradas.titulo: British mum who kept woman as ‘house slave’ for 25 years is jailed
entradas.resumen:
A UK judge on Thursday jailed for 13 years a mother-of-10 who forced a vulnerable woman to work as a “house slave” for more than two decades.
Amanda Wixon subjected the woman, who was a teenager when the ordeal began, to more than 25 years of abuse at her squalid home in Gloucestershire, western England.
The 56-year-old forced the victim, who has a learning disability, to do manual labour, regularly assaulted her and deprived her of food and healthcare, it emerged during an earlier 13-day...

entradas.titulo: UK PM Starmer admits his ‘mistake’ in appointing Mandelson as US ambassador
entradas.resumen:
British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has taken responsibility for his “mistake” in sending Peter Mandelson to Washington as ambassador, as Downing Street denied there was a “cover-up” in the release of files related to the appointment.
The prime minister is facing fresh questions about his judgment in giving Mandelson the ambassadorship despite being warned of a “general reputational risk” because of his relationship with paedophile Jeffrey Epstein.
In his first public appearance since the...

entradas.titulo: Strait of Hormuz must stay blocked, Iran’s Mojtaba Khamenei says in defiant statement
entradas.resumen:
Iran’s new supreme leader has vowed to keep blocking the Strait of Hormuz, attack US bases and avenge the blood of its martyrs in his first statement since succeeding his father.
“The lever of blocking the Strait of Hormuz must definitely be used,” Mojtaba Khamenei said about the strategic waterway, which carries about a fifth of global oil shipments.
Khamenei urged Iran’s Gulf neighbours to close US military bases in the region “as soon as possible, because they must have realised by now that...

entradas.titulo: Ant smuggling rises in Kenya as Chinese national caught with 2,238 insects at airport
entradas.resumen:
A man was arrested with more ⁠than 2,200 live ⁠garden ants in his ⁠luggage at Nairobi’s main airport this week amid a rise in cases of smuggling of the insects in Kenya.
Chinese national Zhang Kequn, 27, was arrested at Jomo Kenyatta ‌International Airport on Tuesday while he was trying to leave the country, court filings on Thursday showed. Immigration officials flagged a “stop order” on Zhang’s passport after he evaded arrest in Kenya last year.
Ant aficionados pay large sums to ⁠maintain...

entradas.titulo: American military bleeds US$11.3 billion in first week of Iran war: report
entradas.resumen:
The opening week of the war against Iran cost the United States more than US$11.3 billion, lawmakers were told in a Pentagon briefing, according to a New York Times report underscoring the pace at which the conflict is consuming weapons and resources.
The Times, citing unnamed sources familiar with Tuesday’s closed-door briefing, said members of Congress were told that the figure excludes many costs connected with the build-up to the strikes – suggesting the final tally for the first week could...

entradas.titulo: From AirAsia to Qantas, airlines raise fares as Iran war fuels oil price surge
entradas.resumen:
Airlines around the world are increasing fares and raising fuel surcharges as the Iran war sends oil prices swinging wildly and stokes fears of jet fuel shortages if the fighting persists.
The cost of plane tickets may jump as much as 9 per cent, said Willie Walsh, director general of the International Air Transport Association industry group. Fuel costs account for about a quarter of the expenses paid by carriers.
Demand for alternative routes that bypass the Middle East is also surging,...

entradas.titulo: US-Israeli war on Iran may spark nuclear arms race, Carnegie scholar says
entradas.resumen:
Zhao Tong is a senior fellow with the Nuclear Policy Programme at Washington-based think tank the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace and its East Asia-based research centre on contemporary China, Carnegie China.
His research focuses on strategic security issues, including nuclear weapons policy, deterrence, arms control, non-proliferation, missile defence, hypersonic weapons, regional security in the Asia-Pacific and China’s security and foreign policy.
SCMP Plus readers get early access...

entradas.titulo: Life under fire: a Tehran resident shares her story as Iran is attacked
entradas.resumen:
A Tehran resident in her 30s agreed to share her thoughts with Agence France-Presse about the ongoing war and daily life.
Her identity has been withheld for her protection. Here is an edited transcript of the conversation.
How is daily life in Tehran?
People left in waves, especially those who were next to targets.
The financial situation is very bad. My job has been halted and I am spending out of my savings. Going away has costs too, so this may be one of the reasons why people are leaving...

entradas.titulo: 6,000 Filipino sailors stranded by Strait of Hormuz closure: ‘bored and a little scared’
entradas.resumen:
Filipino sailor George Miranda was racing to help a stricken vessel aboard the tugboat Mussafah 2 when he last spoke to his wife and young daughter.
The 46-year-old, whose small ship was struck by a pair of missiles this week in the Strait of Hormuz, is the only seafarer from the Philippines known to be missing in the Middle East war, the government says.
But more than 6,000 others from the country that supplies a quarter of the world’s sailors are still working in the conflict zone and...

entradas.titulo: Trump’s ‘racist hate speech’ fuelling rights violations in US, UN panel says
entradas.resumen:
A UN-backed panel of independent experts focusing on racial discrimination has said that racist hate speech by US President Donald Trump and other American political leaders, along with a crackdown on immigration in the United States, have led to “grave human rights violations”.
The Geneva-based Committee on the Elimination of Racial Discrimination issued its decision on Wednesday and urged the US to suspend immigration enforcement operations at, and near, schools, hospitals, and faith-based...

entradas.titulo: ‘Outdated intelligence’: US faces mounting evidence in deadly strike on Iran school
entradas.resumen:
Outdated intelligence likely led to the United States carrying out a deadly missile strike on a junior school in Iran that killed over 165 people, many of them children, in the opening hours of the conflict, according to a US official and a second person briefed on findings of a preliminary US military investigation into the incident.
The bombing of the school and its casualties involving children has become a focal point of the war, and if ultimately confirmed to be at the hands of the US,...

entradas.titulo: Preparing for the worst? US bunker maker claims big demand in Middle East
entradas.resumen:
Since the war in the Middle East began nearly two weeks ago, the phone at Ron Hubbard’s bomb shelter company in Texas has not stopped ringing.
Foreign and US clients are rushing to buy his bunkers, seeking refuge in case of air raids, nuclear fallout or apocalypse.
With the United States and Israel pounding Iran, and Tehran retaliating with strikes across the region, Hubbard has seen demand for his product soar, mostly from Gulf nation clients in Bahrain, Qatar, Kuwait and the United Arab...
